principle: No workstream passes from documentation or placeholder artifacts. Every automated assertion must inspect repository state or a measured evidence record.
business_goal: Determine whether Hyper can produce and maintain a small evidence-grounded static site with fewer defects or lower lifecycle cost than a direct typed-content and template implementation.

W1.goal: Establish honest package ownership and independent consumption before product proof.

failure_vectors.vector: Unclassified reference source silently remains canonical
failure_vectors.attack: Add a new reference/src file without inventory entry
failure_vectors.assertion: inventory coverage equals filesystem coverage

failure_vectors.vector: One source file is assigned to multiple owners
failure_vectors.attack: Duplicate path across ownership records
failure_vectors.assertion: every path has exactly one owner

failure_vectors.vector: Hyper Site public entrypoint still reaches reference runtime code
failure_vectors.attack: Re-export or dynamic import from reference/dist or reference/src
failure_vectors.assertion: runtime dependency scan contains zero Hyper Site to reference edges

failure_vectors.vector: Hyper Content stable exports still reach reference runtime code
failure_vectors.attack: Compatibility export is mislabeled stable
failure_vectors.assertion: stable dependency scan contains zero Hyper Content to reference edges

failure_vectors.vector: Duplicate canonical compiler implementations drift
failure_vectors.attack: Copy compiler while retaining executable legacy implementation
failure_vectors.assertion: one canonical implementation and reference consumes package output

failure_vectors.vector: Packed package succeeds only inside monorepo
failure_vectors.attack: Consumer resolves workspace-relative files or undeclared dependencies
failure_vectors.assertion: two consumers install tarballs in isolated temporary directories

failure_vectors.vector: Extraction changes generated artifacts or rejection behavior
failure_vectors.attack: Route, escaping, ordering, metadata, hash, or error-text drift
failure_vectors.assertion: fixture HTML, Markdown, sitemap, dependency index, page hash, build hash, and negative test match approved baseline

failure_vectors.vector: Generated dist is committed but source owner is false
failure_vectors.attack: Public entrypoint targets copied dist with no package-owned source
failure_vectors.assertion: entrypoint target is produced from owning package src and source manifest records provenance

failure_vectors.vector: Experimental code leaks into stable API
failure_vectors.attack: Star export exposes ontology, vector, Wasm, GPU, or acceleration modules
failure_vectors.assertion: stable exports are explicit and experimental exports are separately named

failure_vectors.vector: Inventory becomes stale after rename or deletion
failure_vectors.attack: Inventory contains paths absent from filesystem
failure_vectors.assertion: orphan inventory entries equal zero

W2.goal: Prove a clean consumer can build five distinct static pages and compare honestly with a direct control.

failure_vectors.vector: W2 starts before W1 passes
failure_vectors.attack: Five-page fixture imports repository internals
failure_vectors.assertion: W2 runner refuses execution unless signed W1 report is green for current commit

failure_vectors.vector: Five routes are aliases or duplicated content
failure_vectors.attack: Different paths render the same canonical question and module graph
failure_vectors.assertion: routes, canonical questions, titles, and page-purpose fingerprints are distinct

failure_vectors.vector: Consumer relies on undeclared monorepo paths
failure_vectors.attack: Relative imports escape consumer root
failure_vectors.assertion: isolated packed consumer has zero imports outside its root except installed packages

failure_vectors.vector: Determinism is cosmetic
failure_vectors.attack: Hashes exclude sitemap, instructions, assets, or generated metadata
failure_vectors.assertion: two clean builds are byte-identical across the declared artifact manifest

failure_vectors.vector: Theme hides semantic or accessibility defects
failure_vectors.attack: Visual CSS passes while heading order, landmarks, focus, contrast, or reduced motion fails
failure_vectors.assertion: critical accessibility findings equal zero and static HTML remains complete

failure_vectors.vector: Direct control is intentionally weaker
failure_vectors.attack: Control lacks equivalent routes, content, metadata, assets, validation, or output requirements
failure_vectors.assertion: frozen equivalence manifest matches both implementations

failure_vectors.vector: Inspect command reports declared rather than emitted state
failure_vectors.attack: Manifest says five pages while dist contains fewer or stale pages
failure_vectors.assertion: inspection derives results from emitted artifact bytes

failure_vectors.vector: Retired or noindex pages leak into sitemap
failure_vectors.attack: Indexability metadata and sitemap diverge
failure_vectors.assertion: sitemap set equals emitted indexable canonical URL set

failure_vectors.vector: Developer workflow only works on one machine
failure_vectors.attack: Implicit shell tools, global packages, locale, timezone, or path assumptions
failure_vectors.assertion: environment record and portable Node commands are required

failure_vectors.vector: Useful-page claim is inferred from successful rendering
failure_vectors.attack: Five syntactically valid but unjustified pages pass
failure_vectors.assertion: each page has a distinct user task, approved evidence, supported claim, and acceptance decision

W3.goal: Measure whether Hyper reduces maintenance defects or effort versus the equivalent direct control.

failure_vectors.vector: Expected affected-page set is defined after seeing output
failure_vectors.attack: Move goalposts to make dependency index appear correct
failure_vectors.assertion: scenario manifest is frozen before execution

failure_vectors.vector: Hash-only comparison misses semantic defects
failure_vectors.attack: Changed output has expected hash set but wrong facts or links
failure_vectors.assertion: byte diff, semantic assertions, and affected-page set all pass

failure_vectors.vector: Shared fact fails to propagate everywhere
failure_vectors.attack: One duplicated literal remains stale
failure_vectors.assertion: all declared dependents contain the new fact and no nondependents change

failure_vectors.vector: Page-specific change causes broad rebuild or content drift
failure_vectors.attack: Global ordering or nondeterminism touches unrelated pages
failure_vectors.assertion: actual changed pages equal frozen expected set

failure_vectors.vector: Shared design change corrupts content or metadata
failure_vectors.attack: Renderer change modifies claims, canonicals, or indexability
failure_vectors.assertion: design scenario changes only declared presentation bytes

failure_vectors.vector: Retired page remains discoverable
failure_vectors.attack: HTML removed but sitemap, internal link, instruction file, or dependency entry remains
failure_vectors.assertion: retirement removes every declared discovery surface

failure_vectors.vector: Invalid change partially writes output
failure_vectors.attack: Compiler emits some files before rejection
failure_vectors.assertion: failed build leaves prior artifact manifest unchanged and emits no mixed state

failure_vectors.vector: Operator time excludes repair or setup
failure_vectors.attack: Measure only successful command duration
failure_vectors.assertion: clock includes edit, run, diagnosis, correction, rerun, and rollback

failure_vectors.vector: Framework advantage is claimed from an invariant the control was not allowed to implement
failure_vectors.attack: Asymmetric validation requirements
failure_vectors.assertion: control may implement equivalent direct checks and comparison records their cost

failure_vectors.vector: No advantage is found but expansion continues
failure_vectors.attack: Treat neutral or losing result as roadmap success
failure_vectors.assertion: failed advantage gate produces stop-or-narrow decision
